title: Podman: Recommended space for /usr ?
body:
Folks, I'm kind of desperate.

I'm getting the following error when I try to PULL an image to my server:

podman pull icr.io/ibmz/ibmz-optimized-for-tensorflow:1.0
Trying to pull icr.io/ibmz/ibmz-optimized-for-tensorflow:1.0...
Getting image source signatures

Copying blob 69150699893a done  
Copying config 0bcbb0ac98 done  
Writing manifest to image destination

  ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: write /usr/bin/chsh: no space left on device
Error: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:914140142ed8e3ef55ee37db043a980f8731728e56e161aafeb42b7a08ef1ec9": ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: write /usr/bin/chsh: no space left on device

comments.body:
You are using the vfs driver, which is probably using up all of the space on your system.
Make sure you  have fuse-overlayfs installed.  You need to reset your storage, this will remove all containers and images.
$ podman system reset
Then check podman info to make sure you are using the overlay driver
$ podman info

I don't know SLES.
But these two do.

@vrothberg @saschagrunert PTAL

Rootful containers should work with overlay in Podman.  I am not sure if the btrfs driver would work or not.
VFS works everywhere but it is very wasteful on disk space as you are seeing.

If your homedir was created with xfs or BTRFS and reflinks, it could work better.

Hi again Dan, thanks again for the help.

I managed to implement fuse-overlayfs:

aqlinux1:~ # fuse-overlayfs --version
fuse-overlayfs: version 1.1.0
FUSE library version 3.6.1
using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29
aqlinux1:~ #

However the result was the same. 

Storing signatures
  Error processing tar file(exit status 1): write /usr/bin/chsh: no space left on device
Error: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:914140142ed8e3ef55ee37db043a980f8731728e56e161aafeb42b7a08ef1ec9": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): write /usr/bin/chsh: no space left on device.

Dumb question. When you mention "homedir" you refer to the folder where the containers will be hosted, right?

Thanks!

Yes your container content is being stored in 
/home/vezio/.local/share/containers/storage

Did you make sure that the rootless Podman storage driver was set to overlayfs?  You may need to run `podman system reset` before but note that it will remove all local images, containers etc.

Regarding fuse-overlayfs on SLES.  I recommend reaching out through the SUSE customer channels (e.g., Bugzilla).  There is nothing we can do upstream.
Thanks for all the clarifications @vrothberg  and @rhatdan  I appreciate that!
